fields.description: Master switch for the Domain Enrichment feature.
fields.label: Enabled

fields.description: Automatically enrich a CRM Organization in the background as soon as it is created (requires a website). When off, enrichment is triggered manually via the Enrich button.
fields.label: Auto-enrich new Organizations

fields.description: Maximum number of pages crawled per enrichment.
fields.label: Max Pages

fields.description: Maximum link depth followed from the homepage.
fields.label: Max Depth

fields.description: Seed the crawl queue from the site's sitemap (declared in robots.txt, or the conventional /sitemap.xml) so pages not reachable within Max Depth still get a chance to be crawled. This is an addition on top of ordinary link-following, not a replacement -- a site with no sitemap falls straight through to link-following with no error.
fields.label: Use Sitemap

fields.description: Maximum bytes downloaded per page (the stream is capped).
fields.label: Max Download Bytes

fields.description: Number of retries on a failed request.
fields.label: Retry Count

fields.default: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/125.0.0.0 Safari/537.36
fields.label: User Agent

fields.description: Pages crawled for the fast, synchronous create-time prefill.
fields.label: Preview Max Pages

fields.description: Per-request timeout in seconds for the preview prefill.
fields.label: Preview Timeout (s)

fields.description: Crawl-ordering hints (not extraction). Links whose URL path or anchor text contain one of these are fetched first; higher weight = sooner. Lets the crawler spend its page budget on company pages (about/contact/team) before the cap. Defaults are seeded; edit or remove as needed.
fields.label: Link Priority Order

fields.description: Substrings that exclude a candidate link from the crawl.
fields.label: Skip Patterns

fields.description: Allowlist (subdomain-aware). If you add any domains here, the crawler may ONLY visit those domains and their subdomains — everything else is rejected by the SSRF guard. Leave empty (the normal case) to allow any public site; this is for locked-down deployments that should reach only specific domains.
fields.label: Allowed Domains

fields.description: Denylist (subdomain-aware). A host that equals or is a subdomain of any entry is always rejected by the SSRF guard — e.g. blocking "acme.com" also blocks "shop.acme.com". Use it to permanently exclude internal domains, known-bad hosts, or link shorteners. Always wins over the allowlist.
fields.label: Blocked Domains
